### Post by sdowney717 on 2012-03-07
xorg.conf file is saved by nvidia-settings
yet the system always boots with a 60 hz refresh
so I have to run nvidia-settings and force set to 85 every single time.
shows an 85 is saved in the xorg
"metamodes" "1600x1200_85 +0+0; 1600x1200 +0+0"

> ```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 295.20  (buildd@rothera)  Sat Feb 18 09:49:17 UTC 2012


Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Sony CPD-E540"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     48.0 - 170.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 8400 GS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1600x1200_85 +0+0; 1600x1200 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

### Post by dino99 on 2012-03-07
X is now drived by the kernel and as you might know nvidia-settings is from "nvidia" so the few conflicts around

about refresh rates: the kernel detect your hardware and adjust to the best stable refresh rate : display vs graphic/chipset video

its up to you to check the available refresh rates of your hardware. By the way is 60 hz really an issue ?

---

### Post by sdowney717 on 2012-03-07
it is with my CRT monitor.
yes it hurts my eyes.

With the prior ubuntu version, it remembered this setting.
Makes me wonder if other settings are going to be lost on rebooting.

---

### Post by sdowney717 on 2012-03-07
interesting that you say it adjusts to the best refresh rate. Maybe that would be true if it was an LCD monitor. Since it is a CRT, 60hz is not the best refresh rate. It has an EDID that comes back from monitor, so it can know what this is and it is not setting the refresh to the best setting. So it seems kinda unintelligent kernel to me. Makes me think it is assuming everyone has LCD and therefore just set refresh to 60, which takes no brains at all.

I dont recall ever telling the system anytime that it is a Sony CPD-E540

>     # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Sony CPD-E540"

### Post by dino99 on 2012-03-07
have you tried with lower rates ?

HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     48.0 - 170.0

these two seems way to high, try 30.0 - 75.0  &  48.0 - 75.0
you might find something working better

### Post by Grenage on 2012-03-07
> **dino99 said:**
> have you tried with lower rates ?

HorizSync       30.0 - 110.0
    VertRefresh     48.0 - 170.0

these two seems way to high, try 30.0 - 75.0  &  48.0 - 75.0
you might find something working better

Those are the correct ranges for that monitor; CRTs have much bigger ranges.

The issue mentioned must be a bug, but does a boot-time xrandr command work?

### Post by sdowney717 on 2012-03-07
no, what good would that do to modify those lines with lower numbers. Doing a search on that verticle refresh line brings up many hits, so this is likely a standard well used xorg line.

I just tried adding a modeline to xorg.conf and it left me with a non booting system.

>  Modeline "1600x1200" 301.81  1600 1752 2120 2808  1200 1200 1204 1264 

Interesting is that it could not even load failsafe graphics! Failsafe graphics ought to ignore xorg.conf completely, IMO.

So I dropped to root login and deleted xorg.conf and now I am posting this message.

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules/+bug/89640](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules/+bug/89640)

look at #4 post, you can get a modeline generator but I have no idea if it is still valid or quite how to work it into xorg.conf

---

### Post by Grenage on 2012-03-07
> **sdowney717 said:**
> no, what good would that do to modify those lines with lower numbers. Doing a search on that verticle refresh line brings up many hits, so this is likely a standard well used xorg line.

I just tried adding a modeline to xorg.conf and it left me with a non booting system.



Interesting is that it could not even load failsafe graphics! Failsafe graphics ought to ignore xorg.conf completely, IMO.

So I dropped to root login and deleted xorg.conf and now I am posting this message.

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules/+bug/89640](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-restricted-modules/+bug/89640)

look at #4 post, you can get a modeline generator but I have no idea if it is still valid or quite how to work it into xorg.conf

It should still work, and would be:

```
Modeline "1600x1200_85.00"  235.00  1600 1728 1896 2192  1200 1203 1207 1262 -hsync +vsync
```

Put it directly under the monitor frequency ranges, it needs to be in the monitor section.

---

### Post by dino99 on 2012-03-07
here is an old working xorg.conf (no more used now anyway)


Section "Module"
EndSection

Section	"Monitor"
	Identifier	"Monitor0"
	VendorName	"GSM"
	ModelName	"22LS4R"
	HorizSync	28.0 - 83.0
	VertRefresh	58.0 - 63.0
	Option		"dpms"
	Modeline	"1680x1050_100"	146.25	1680	1728	1760	1840	1050	1053	1059	1089	-HSync	+VSync
Endsection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Default Device"
	Driver	"nvidia"
	Option	"NoLogo"	"True"
EndSection


So your CRT can work with high rates, but does your graphic card/chipset is able to work with such rates ? Look at its available rates range by definition (the higher definition used, then the lower rate possible, eg 800x600_100 -> 1920x1080_60 (for example))

---

### Post by cariboo on 2012-03-07
I see you are using a CRT, you have to set the Horizontal and Vertical refresh rates to what is specified in the owners manual. I had a look at the Sony website, and the manual states the refresh rates should be:

[LIST]
[*]Horizontal  30 -70
[*]Vertical         48-120
[/LIST]

If you set the refresh rates to the above, you should be able to save the rate you want.

---

    # generated from default

    Driver         "kbd"

    # generated from default

    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"

    HorizSync       30.0 - 96.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 160.0

    BoardName      "GeForce GT 240"
EndSection

Section "Screen"

# Removed Option "metamodes" "1280x1024 +0+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "1600x1200_75 +0+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "1280x1024 +0+0"
# Removed Option "metamodes" "1280x1024_85 +0+0; 1280x1024 +0+0"

    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "TwinViewXineramaInfoOrder" "CRT-1"
    Option         "metamodes" "1280x1024_85 +0+0; 1280x1024 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```

I want my resolution to be 1280x1024 by 85 Hz but every time the system boots the refresh rate keeps going back to 60 Hz and I have to set it to 85 Hz in Nvidia X Server Settings...

### Post by sdowney717 on 2012-03-07
I opened a bug report, so if you want to join and add your input feel free.

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/nvidia-settings/+bug/949071](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/nvidia-settings/+bug/949071)

Oddly, adding that last modeline shifted the entire desktop to the left, enough so that left margin was 1/2 inch off the screen.

### Post by dino99 on 2012-03-07
> **sdowney717 said:**
> I opened a bug report, so if you want to join and add your input feel free.

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/nvidia-settings/+bug/949071](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/nvidia-settings/+bug/949071)

Oddly, adding that last modeline shifted the entire desktop to the left, enough so that left margin was 1/2 inch off the screen.

be sure of your settings, nvidia-settings is often faulty, xorg.0.log might help you.

[http://www.mythtv.org/wiki/Modeline_Database](http://www.mythtv.org/wiki/Modeline_Database)
